quote_of_day.text: I still don't understand why Animal was hated so much. I enjoyed it more than Dhurandhar, Animal had more literally me moments with that swastika scene. Animal feels more timeless, and a very very very good story on toxic father etc situations. It was a fantastic movie
quote_of_day.name: Anon

guest_letters.title: Based website
guest_letters.body: Why aren’t we talking more about this IAC guy?? I just woke uop and checked bahut interesting site hai, there’s quotes and funny articles and board status?? We need more anons like iac to make cool shit. Im a very impressed pajeet. Writing this to see if it will get featured on the newspaper or not, im not sure how he set it up but if the !thing works then hello indus news! Im on da news niggaaaaaa
guest_letters.name: Anon

articles.title: Weather for Indus today
articles.body:
A low-pressure system of racial slurs moved into the board, bringing with it a squall of insults comparing Nigerians to Homo Erectus. This sparked a heated debate, drawing in numerous responses.

A front of criticism of Indian leadership and development moved in, marked by complaints about the focus on caste and reservation policies rather than industrial development and research.

This front was accompanied by scattered showers of frustration with the current political climate.

A fog of speculation rolled in around Motivanon's doxxing and board ownership, with one anon seeking clarification on the status of a user named Bharatchan and the ownership of the board.

Meanwhile, a high-pressure system of nationalist sentiment sat over the board, with some posters speculating on the relationship between Indian nationalism and Pakistan's existence.

A brief but intense downpour of praise for the Bollywood film 'Animal' also occurred, with one viewer defending it against criticism.

articles.title: Anon compares Nigerians to Homo Erectus, Sparks Debate
articles.body:
Anon posted on the board, "Chaddi mein coom nikal gaya mera darr ke maare. They are so unhinged, man. Imagine if they flood your country.

They have 0 impulse control like a homo erectus." This remark drew several responses from other users. One anon countered with a historical perspective, mentioning, "I learnt about that russian alliance stuff in a documentary on Central African republic war.

Still its well known, nigger leaders are often incompetent af.

They are corrupt and have said outrageous shit and have taken dumb decisions." Another user chimed in, "That's all fine," and proceeded to make a generalization about "nigger societies." Other posts took a more graphic and disturbing turn.

One user wrote about fantasies involving Candace Owens and sexual violence. Another user fantasized about Israel's actions against Nigerians, stating, "Israel raped niggers too. Learn about Operation Jonathon \"Entebbe raid\".

They raped whole ugandan monkey military and rescued jews from idi amin faggot." Anon also discussed the idea of race purity, with one user writing, "Even a little contamination with negro blood can bring down the mean iq of a race, see brazil." Another user suggested that certain individuals should be "wiped out of their racial gene pool" for having "self destructive empathy." The conversation also touched on the topic of national and ethnic comparisons, with one user stating, "India is objectively far better than subsaharan africa, retard.

India is a low middle income country with nukes, a space program and has accomplished so much despite geopolitical challenges and lacking in natural resources." A respondent fired back, "Defending niggers is genuinely some of the lowest of low caste subhuman roach hill to die on." Anons also brought up historical and geopolitical points, including the idea that "niggers had 10,000 years to catch up" and that certain groups had been in contact with more advanced civilizations through trade.

The discussion remained highly contentious and critical of people of African descent.

articles.title: Criticism of Indian Leadership and Development
articles.body:
Anon criticized Indian leaders, saying they spend too much energy on scams, caste votebank warfare, and "hisab chukta" politics instead of building factories, manufacturing, and R&D. This, according to Anon, is why India remains a laughingstock, still importing everything.

Another anon responded, suggesting that if Varna were truly by choice, ethnic groups that excel in certain fields might abandon the nation.

Anon argued that if India continues down this path, it could lose the faith and loyalty of its best citizens. Anon also discussed the potential economic benefits of eliminating certain schemes, estimating that up to 100B$ could be saved.

This money could, in theory, be used to modernize and arm the military. However, others pointed out that India's military capability isn't inferior, but rather, it needs better leadership and strategy.

Some anons expressed frustration with the current state of India's infrastructure, sewage management, and rivers. They argued that the government is not doing enough to address these issues, and that the population is growing out of control.

Conspiracy theories also emerged, with one anon suggesting that the Indian government might be infiltrated and backed by the CIA to keep the country unstable.

Others discussed the possibility of foreign influence on Indian politics and the potential for India to become a global economic superpower.

The conversation also turned to the topic of military modernization, with anons discussing the need for India to develop its military capabilities. Some argued that with proper investment and strategy, India could become a major military power.

articles.title: Anon Speculates on Motivanon's Doxxing and Board Ownership
articles.body:
Anon questioned what happened with 'bharatchan' and why 'gayush dalit' it, also inquiring about the owner of the board.

Anon explained that the previous owner, presumably 'bharatchan' or 'motivanon', had issues with hosting and reports, which included 'trannies, glowies, paki raids, internal caste beefs', making it difficult to maintain.

A respondent claimed that 'motivanon' had referenced a workplace conversation and his last name in a thread, making it easy to doxx him.

Another anon mentioned that 'motivanon' was doxxed after threats to email his Japanese workplace and harass him with an Excel sheet of girls he interacted with.

Anon expressed regret over doxxing 'motivanon', with one stating, 'We just doxxed him because we could and it was funny.

Kinda regret it tho tbh now.' Speculation arose about 'motivanon's' identity, with some believing he was a Muslim trying to incite civil war in India, and potentially a state-backed asset.

The conversation also touched on 'swissfag shiller vegcuckery', another individual of interest, with some identifying him as a Muslim trying to 'shill civil war in india'.

articles.title: Anon Speculates on Indian Nationalism and Pakistan's Existence
articles.body:
Anon opened the thread by stating that if Pakistan were to vanish, Indian patriotic zeal would plummet to historical lows.

One anon countered that Indians do not obsess over Pakistan, and that nationalism in India would persist without it.

Another anon argued that Pakistan's existence serves as a distraction for India, allowing it to focus on its economy rather than defense and national security.

A respondent claimed that low castes should be kept away from the army and bureaucracy, and that India needs anti-reservation policies.

An anon also brought up the idea that Pakistan's presence allows India to maintain a sense of national identity, but others disputed this notion.

articles.title: Anon Praises Bollywood Film 'Animal'
articles.body:
Anon expressed admiration for the Bollywood film 'Animal', calling it a "fantastic movie" with a "very good story on toxic father etc situations." They enjoyed it more than another film, 'Dhurandhar', and appreciated its "literally me moments", particularly a scene featuring a swastika.

Another anon responded, criticizing those who dislike popular films like 'Animal', labeling them as "midwits" trying to appear intelligent. A different poster discussed caste issues, suggesting that some individuals from lower castes exhibit "dysgenic" tendencies and engage in harmful behavior.

The conversation also touched on the film's portrayal of certain groups, with one anon noting that the protagonists of both 'Animal' and 'Dhurandhar' were from the Jatt Sikh community, which was described as having a history of hostility towards Muslims and Hindus.
